title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: New Graph produces maps across multiple tools: - QGIS — interactive GIS, field data, Mergin Maps sync - tmap — static maps bookdown reports (R) - leaflet — interactive maps bookdown reports (R) - Web mapping — PMTiles + MapLibre GL cloud-native web maps - ggplot2 — statistical plots spatial context (R) Symbology (colors, line weights, labels, classification breaks) currently duplicated manually across tool. Change color QGIS → manually update R code → manually update web styles. doesn’t scale.

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: canonical style registry serves single source truth:
code: QGIS Project (.qgs)          Hand-curated CSV   ↓ gq_qgs_extract()           ↓ gq_reg_read_csv() registry JSON                 registry list   ↓                             ↓   └──── gq_reg_merge() ────────┘               ↓         reg_main.json (master registry)               ↓ gq_reg_main()   ┌───────────┼───────────┐   │ tmap      │ mapgl     │ (future: leaflet, ggplot2)   │ (static)  │ (web)     │   └───────────┴───────────┘

code: {   "layers": {     "watershed": {       "type": "polygon",       "fill": {         "color": "#a8c8e0",         "opacity": 0.4       },       "stroke": {         "color": "#2c3e50",         "width": 1.8       },       "label": {         "field": "name",         "size": 14,         "font": "sans-serif",         "weight": "bold",         "color": "#1a3c5e"       },       "classification": null     },     "parks": {       "type": "polygon",       "fill": {         "color": "#a3c4a3",         "opacity": 0.35       },       "stroke": {         "color": "#5a7a5a",         "width": 0.5       }     },     "streams": {       "type": "line",       "stroke": {         "color": "#7ba7cc",         "width": 0.4       },       "filter": {         "field": "stream_order",         "operator": ">=",         "value": 5       },       "label": {         "field": "gnis_name",         "filter": {"field": "stream_order", "operator": ">=", "value": 7},         "size": 8,         "font": "sans-serif",         "style": "italic",         "color": "#1a5276"       }     },     "roads": {       "type": "line",       "classification": {         "field": "road_type",         "classes": {           "RH1": {"color": "#c0392b", "width": 2.0, "label": "Highway"},           "RA1": {"color": "#e67e22", "width": 1.8, "label": "Arterial"},           "RA2": {"color": "#f1c40f", "width": 1.4, "label": "Secondary"}         }       }     },     "railway": {       "type": "line",       "stroke": {         "color": "#000000",         "width": 1.2       },       "overlay": {         "color": "#ffffff",         "width": 0.6,         "dash": "4 2"       }     }   } }

what: R Package (the package root IS the R package)
title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Registry functions: - gq_reg_main() — load master registry (51+ layers, arguments needed) - gq_registry_read(path) — read registry JSON file - gq_reg_read(path) — alias gq_registry_read() - gq_reg_read_csv(path) — read hand-curated CSV registry - gq_reg_merge(..., csv, priority) — merge multiple registries Style translators: - gq_tmap_style(layer) — translate layer → tmap v4 polygon/line/point args - gq_tmap_classes(layer) — translate classified layer → field, values, labels - gq_mapgl_style(layer) — translate layer → MapLibre GL paint properties - gq_mapgl_classes(layer) — translate classified layer → MapLibre match expression QGIS extraction: - gq_qgs_extract(path) — parse .qgs XML → registry JSON (PyQGIS needed)

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: reg_main.json — master merged registry (single source truth) reg_qgis_restoration.json — extracted restoration QGIS project (47 layers) reg_qgis_fishpassage.json — extracted fish passage QGIS project (42 layers) reg_csv_custom.csv — hand-curated styles layers without QGIS source

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Merges registry sources reg_main.json. Run manually updating source registry.

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Styles data-source-independent. style applies whether layer comes : - PostgreSQL (bcfishpass, fwapg via db-newgraph) - SQLite / SpatiaLite - DuckDB (DuckDB Spatial) - GeoPackage (.gpkg) - Shapefiles / GeoJSON - Cloud-native (PMTiles, COG, FlatGeobuf) registry maps layer names styles, data sources styles. consuming tool (tmap, leaflet, etc.) handles data access separately.

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
code: library(gq) reg <- gq_reg_main()  # load once per script  # Simple layer → tmap sty <- gq_tmap_style(reg$layers$lake) tm_shape(lakes_sf) + do.call(tm_polygons, sty)  # Classified layer → tmap cls <- gq_tmap_classes(reg$layers$pscis_assessment) tm_dots(fill = cls$field, fill.scale = tm_scale_categorical(values = cls$values, labels = cls$labels))  # Simple layer → mapgl lake_gl <- gq_mapgl_style(reg$layers$lake) add_fill_layer(id = "lakes", source = lakes_sf, fill_color = lake_gl$paint[["fill-color"]])  # Classified layer → mapgl bec_expr <- gq_mapgl_classes(reg$layers$bec_zone) add_fill_layer(id = "bec", source = bec_sf, fill_color = bec_expr)

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: gq_reg_merge() controls precedence. Default priority = "last" (later sources win). Projects can merge CSV top gq_reg_main() override specific layers.

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: gq_tmap_classes() auto-converts CAPS fallback labels title case (e.g., BARRIER → Barrier). Explicit label class_label fields registry override — use acronyms like BEC zone codes.

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Teach extreme programming (XP) principles relevant: - YAGNI — Don’t build need - KISS — Simplest solution works - Small commits — Atomic, focused changes - Test early — Verify go <!– BEGIN SOUL CONVENTIONS — EDIT LINE –>

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Checklist effectively running Claude Code agent teams. Agent teams coordinate multiple Claude Code instances working parallel shared tasks inter-agent messaging. Source: code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-teams Status: Experimental. Disabled default.

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Enable feature flag settings.json environment: Choose display mode — -process (default, terminal) split panes (requires tmux iTerm2). Set teammateMode settings.json pass --teammate-mode. Install tmux using split panes (brew install tmux). supported VS Code terminal, Windows Terminal, Ghostty. Pre-approve common permissions permission settings spawning teammates — permission prompts bubble lead create friction.
code: { "env": { "CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS": "1" } }

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Use agent teams teammates need talk — research debates, competing hypotheses, cross-layer coordination. Use subagents just need focused workers report back results. Good fit: parallel code review (security + performance + tests), investigating competing bug hypotheses, new modules don’t share files, research multiple angles. Bad fit: sequential tasks, edits file, simple work coordination overhead exceeds benefit.

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Give enough context spawn prompt — teammates don’t inherit lead’s conversation history. Include file paths, architecture context, specific focus areas. Size tasks right — small = overhead wasted, large = risk divergence. Aim self-contained units clear deliverables. 5-6 tasks per teammate keeps everyone productive. Specify models explicitly want cost control (e.g., “Use Sonnet teammate”). Ensure teammates different files — two teammates editing file leads overwrites.

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Use delegate mode (Shift+Tab) prevent lead implementing tasks instead waiting teammates. Monitor steer — check progress, redirect approaches aren’t working. Don’t let team run unattended long. Message teammates directly — Shift+/-process mode, click pane split mode. Give additional instructions ask follow-ups. lead starts coding instead delegating, tell : “Wait teammates complete tasks proceeding.”

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Require plan approval risky work — teammates plan read-mode lead approves. Give criteria: “approve plans include test coverage.” TeammateIdle — exit code 2 sends feedback keeps teammate working TaskCompleted — exit code 2 prevents completion sends feedback

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Shut teammates first — ask lead: “Ask researcher teammate shut .” Teammates finish current action stopping. clean team — tell lead: “Clean team.” removes shared resources. Cleanup fails teammates still running. Always clean via lead — teammates never run cleanup (team context may resolve correctly). Check orphaned tmux sessions cleanup:
code: tmux ls tmux kill-session -t <session-name>

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: session resumption — /resume /rewind don’t restore -process teammates. resuming, tell lead spawn new teammates. Task status can lag — teammates sometimes fail mark tasks complete, blocking dependents. Manually check update stuck. One team per session — clean starting new team. nested teams — lead can manage team. Lead fixed — can’t promote teammate transfer leadership. Permissions set spawn — teammates inherit lead’s mode. Change individually spawning needed.

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
code: project/ ├── index.Rmd                # Master config, YAML params, setup chunks ├── _bookdown.yml            # book_filename, output_dir: "docs" ├── _output.yml              # Gitbook, pagedown, pdf_book config ├── 0100-intro.Rmd           # Chapter numbering: 4-digit, 100s increment ├── 0200-background.Rmd ├── 0300-methods.Rmd ├── 0400-results.Rmd ├── 0500-*.Rmd               # Discussion/recommendations ├── 0800-appendix-*.Rmd      # Appendices (site-specific in fish passage) ├── 2000-references.Rmd      # Auto-generated from .bib ├── 2090-report-change-log.Rmd  # Auto-generated from NEWS.md ├── 2100-session-info.Rmd    # Reproducibility ├── NEWS.md                  # Changelog (semantic versioning) ├── scripts/ │   ├── packages.R           # Package loading (renv-managed) │   ├── functions.R          # Project-specific functions │   ├── staticimports.R      # Auto-generated from staticimports pkg │   ├── setup_docs.R         # Build helper │   └── run.R                # Local build (gitbook + PDF) ├── fig/                     # Figures (organized by chapter or type) ├── data/                    # Project data ├── docs/                    # Rendered output (GitHub Pages) ├── renv.lock                # Locked dependencies └── .Rprofile                # Activates renv

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Every index.Rmd follows setup sequence. Order matters. Responsive settings output format:
code: # 1. Gitbook vs PDF switch gitbook_on <- TRUE  # 2. Knitr options knitr::opts_chunk$set(   echo = identical(gitbook_on, TRUE),  # Show code only in gitbook   message = FALSE, warning = FALSE,   dpi = 60, out.width = "100%" ) options(scipen = 999) options(knitr.kable.NA = '--') options(knitr.kable.NAN = '--')  # 3. Source in order: packages → static imports → functions → data source('scripts/packages.R') source('scripts/staticimports.R') source('scripts/functions.R') # Gitbook photo_width <- "100%"; font_set <- 11  # PDF (paged.js) photo_width <- "80%"; font_set <- 9

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Parameters live index.Rmd frontmatter (separate file). Child repos override editing values. Fish passage repos add project-specific params (project_region, model_species, wsg_code, update flags forms). project-specific — don’t add general template.
code: params:   repo_url: 'https://github.com/NewGraphEnvironment/repo_name'   report_url: 'https://www.newgraphenvironment.com/repo_name/'   update_packages: FALSE   update_bib: TRUE   gitbook_on: TRUE

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Embed context purpose chunk names. principle universal; codes project-specific. Pattern: {type}-{system}-{description}

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Bookdown auto-prepends fig: tab: chunk names. Tables: Table \@ref(tab:chunk-name) Figures: Figure \@ref(fig:chunk-name) fig: tab: prefix chunk label — bookdown adds .

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Interactive tables (DT) can’t use standard bookdown captions. Use my_tab_caption() function staticimports.R. Pattern: Separate -cap chunk table chunk. my_tab_caption() auto-grabs chunk label via knitr::opts_current$get()$label wraps HTML caption tags bookdown can cross-reference.
code: # Caption chunk — must use results="asis" {r tab-sites-sum-cap, results="asis"} my_caption <- "Summary of fish passage assessment procedures." my_tab_caption() # Table chunk — renders the DT {r tab-sites-sum} data |> my_dt_table(page_length = 20, cols_freeze_left = 0)

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Separate prep chunk (find file) display chunk (render ).
code: # Prep — find the photo {r photo-196332-01-prep} my_photo1 <- fpr::fpr_photo_pull_by_str(str_to_pull = 'ds_typical_1_') my_caption1 <- paste0('Typical habitat downstream of PSCIS crossing ', my_site, '.') # Gitbook — full width {r photo-196332-01, fig.cap=my_caption1, out.width=photo_width, eval=gitbook_on} knitr::include_graphics(my_photo1) # PDF — side by side with 1% spacer {r photo-196332-d01, fig.show="hold", out.width=c("49.5%","1%","49.5%"), eval=identical(gitbook_on, FALSE)} knitr::include_graphics(my_photo1) knitr::include_graphics("fig/pixel.png") knitr::include_graphics(my_photo2)

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Use rbbt (Better BibTeX) dynamically generate references.bib Zotero. Auto-generate package citations: Use nocite: YAML include references cited text.
code: bibliography: "`r rbbt::bbt_write_bib('references.bib', overwrite = TRUE)`" biblio-style: apalike link-citations: no knitr::write_bib(c(.packages(), 'bookdown', 'knitr', 'rmarkdown'), 'packages.bib')

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: single boolean gitbook_on controls output format throughout.
code: # Show only in gitbook {r map-interactive, eval=gitbook_on}  # Show only in PDF {r fig-print-only, eval=identical(gitbook_on, FALSE)}  # Conditional inline content `r if(identical(gitbook_on, FALSE)) knitr::asis_output("This report is available online...")`  # Page breaks for PDF only `r if(gitbook_on){knitr::asis_output("")} else knitr::asis_output("\\pagebreak")`

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Reports use MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH versioning NEWS.md changelog. Version index.Rmd YAML: NEWS.md format: Auto-append appendix via my_news_to_appendix() staticimports.R: Convention: - Bump version index.Rmd add NEWS entry every commit main changes report content - Tag releases: git tag -v1.1.0 -m "v1.1.0: Brief description" - MAJOR: structural changes, new chapters, methodology changes - MINOR: new content, figures, tables, discussion sections - PATCH: prose fixes, corrections, formatting
code: date: |  |  | Version 1.1.0 DRAFT `r format(Sys.Date(), "%Y-%m-%d")` ## 1.1.0 (2026-02-17)  - Add feature X - Fix issue Y ([Issue #N](https://github.com/Org/repo/issues/N)) news_to_appendix(md_name = "NEWS.md", rmd_name = "2090-report-change-log.Rmd")

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Always use ngr::ngr_str_viewer_cog() — never hardcode viewer iframes. function includes cache-busting ?v= parameter. Bump v function default viewer.html breaking changes.
code: knitr::asis_output(ngr::ngr_str_viewer_cog("https://bucket.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/ortho.tif"))

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Use renv reproducible package management: - .Rprofile activates renv startup - renv::restore() installs lockfile - renv::snapshot() updates lockfile adding packages - Use pak::pak("pkg") install (install.packages)

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Production repos (2024-2025) drifted templates areas. working child repo, match repo , template: Script naming 02_reporting/ — older repos use tables.R, 0165-read-sqlite.R; newer repos use numbered 0130-tables.R. Follow repo ’re . Removed packages — elevatr, rayshader, arrow removed production still template. staticimports::import() call — repos skip source staticimports.R directly. Hardcoded vs parameterized years — older repos hardcode years file paths; newer repos use params$project_year. Prefer parameterized.

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Three levels. Default casual unless context dictates otherwise. Collaborative, directive. Acknowledge constraints: Avoid: “Work makes sense lab” Better: “’re able work fits schedule really helpful”

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Draft markdown, convert HTML send time via gmailr. See compost script templates, OAuth setup, search_gmail.R. File naming: YYYYMMDD_recipient_topic_draft.md + YYYYMMDD_recipient_topic.R Key gotchas (documented detail compost): - Gmail strips <style> blocks — use inline styles tables - gm_create_draft() support thread_id — gm_send_message() can reply threads. Drafts land outside conversation. - Always use test_mode create_draft variables safe workflows

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Never manually type data tables — generate programmatically source files Link canonical sources (GitHub repos, public reports) rather embedding raw data Provide CSV Excel sharing tabular data Document ID codes — using compressed IDs (e.g., id_lab), include reference sheet recipients can decode

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Behavioral guidelines reduce common LLM coding mistakes. Merge project-specific instructions needed. Tradeoff: guidelines bias toward caution speed. trivial tasks, use judgment.

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Don’t assume. Don’t hide confusion. Surface tradeoffs. implementing: - State assumptions explicitly. uncertain, ask. - multiple interpretations exist, present - don’t pick silently. - simpler approach exists, say . Push back warranted. - something unclear, stop. Name ’s confusing. Ask.

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Minimum code solves problem. Nothing speculative. features beyond asked. abstractions single-use code. “flexibility” “configurability” wasn’t requested. error handling impossible scenarios. write 200 lines 50, rewrite . Ask : “senior engineer say overcomplicated?” yes, simplify.

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Touch must. Clean mess. editing existing code: - Don’t “improve” adjacent code, comments, formatting. - Don’t refactor things aren’t broken. - Match existing style, even ’d differently. - notice unrelated dead code, mention - don’t delete . changes create orphans: - Remove imports/variables/functions changes made unused. - Don’t remove pre-existing dead code unless asked. test: Every changed line trace directly user’s request.

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Define success criteria. Loop verified. Transform tasks verifiable goals: - “Add validation” → “Write tests invalid inputs, make pass” - “Fix bug” → “Write test reproduces , make pass” - “Refactor X” → “Ensure tests pass ” multi-step tasks, state brief plan: Strong success criteria let loop independently. Weak criteria (“make work”) require constant clarification. guidelines working : fewer unnecessary changes diffs, fewer rewrites due overcomplication, clarifying questions come implementation rather mistakes.
code: 1. [Step] → verify: [check] 2. [Step] → verify: [check] 3. [Step] → verify: [check]

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Check duplicates: gh issue list --state open --search "<keywords>" – search creating Link SRED: work involves infrastructure, R&D, tooling, performance benchmarking, add Relates NewGraphEnvironment/sred-2025-2026#N (match repo name SRED issue title) One issue, one concern. Keep focused.

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Write issues clear technical focus: Use normal technical language titles descriptions Focus problem solution approach Add tracking links end (e.g., Relates Owner/repo#N) Issue body structure:
code: ## Problem <what's wrong or missing>  ## Proposed Solution <approach>  Relates to #<local> Relates to NewGraphEnvironment/sred-2025-2026#<N>

what: GitHub Issue Creation - Always Use Files
title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: gh issue create command heredoc syntax fails repeatedly EOF errors. ALWAYS use --body-file:
code: cat > /tmp/issue_body.md << 'EOF' ## Problem ...  ## Proposed Solution ... EOF  gh issue create --title "Brief technical title" --body-file /tmp/issue_body.md

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: : Close issues via commit messages. commit closure documentation. DON’T: Close issues gh issue close. breaks audit trail — ’s linked diff showing changed. Fixes #N Closes #N — auto-closes links commit issue Relates #N — partial progress, close Always close issues work complete. Don’t leave stale open issues.
code: Fix broken DEM path in loading pipeline  Update hardcoded path to use config-driven resolution.  Fixes #20 Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Write clear, informative commit messages: commit: - Logical, atomic units work - Working state (tests pass) - Clear description changes avoid: - “WIP” “temp” commits main branch - Combining unrelated changes - Vague messages like “fixes” “updates”
code: Brief description (50 chars or less)  Detailed explanation of changes and impact.  Fixes #<issue> (or Relates to #<issue>) Relates to NewGraphEnvironment/sred-2025-2026#<N>  Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Rules learned real project sessions. apply across repos. Cite primary sources — review paper references older study, trace back original cite . Don’t attribute findings review original exists. Install missing packages, don’t workaround — package needed, ask user install (e.g. pak::pak("pkg")). Don’t write degraded fallback code avoid dependency. Never hardcode extractable data — coordinates, station names, metadata can pulled API database runtime, . Don’t hardcode values programmatic source. Close issues via commits, gh issue close — see Closing Issues .

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Pattern: noun_verb-detail – noun first, verb second across naming: Scripts logs live together: scripts/<module>/logs/

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Projects = daily cross-repo tracking (always add relevant project) Milestones = iteration boundaries (release/claim prep) Don’t double-track unless ’s reason

text: tidyverse style guide: snake_case, pipe operators (|> %>%) Match existing patterns codebase Use pak package installation (install.packages)

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Follow R Packages (2e) conventions: - R/ functions, tests/testthat/ tests, man/ docs - DESCRIPTION proper fields (Title, Description, Authors@R) - NAMESPACE managed roxygen2 (#' @export, #' @importFrom) - Never edit NAMESPACE man/ hand

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Use testthat 3e (Config/testthat/edition: 3 DESCRIPTION) Run devtools::test() committing Test files mirror source: R/utils.R -> tests/testthat/test-utils.R Use testthat::snapshot_test() complex outputs

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Run lintr::lint_package() committing R package code. Fix warnings — every lint worth fixing.

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: 120 char line length (default 80 strict data pipelines) Allow dotted.case (common base R legacy code) Suppress commented code lints (exploratory R scripts often commented alternatives) Exclude renv directory entirely
code: linters: linters_with_defaults(     line_length_linter(120),     object_name_linter(styles = c("snake_case", "dotted.case")),     commented_code_linter = NULL   ) exclusions: list(     "renv" = list(linters = "all")   )

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: roxygen2 exported functions @examples non-trivial functions Vignettes workflows, just API reference pkgdown site package public

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Minimize Imports — use Suggests packages needed tests/vignettes Pin versions breaking changes known Prefer packages already tidyverse ecosystem

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: references flow Claude Code, Zotero, technical writing New Graph Environment.

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Three tools, different purposes. Use right one. Citation keys vs item keys: Citation keys (like irvine2020ParsnipRiver) come Better BibTeX. Item keys (like K7WALMSY) native Zotero. MCP works item keys. /zotero-lookup bridges citation keys item data. BBT database location: BBT migrated better-bibtex.sqlite better-bibtex.migrated (Feb 2025+). old .sqlite file stale — always use better-bibtex.migrated citation key lookups. better-bibtex-search.sqlite also stale unrelated.

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: research turns reference: - DOI available: Tell user — Zotero’s magic wand (DOI lookup) fastest path - ResearchGate link: Flag user manual check — programmatic fetch blocked (403), full text often - BC gov report: Search ACAT, .gov.bc.ca library, EIRS viewer - Paywalled: Note , move . Don’t waste time trying bypass.

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: Preferred order: 1. DOI magic wand Zotero UI (fastest, complete metadata) 2. saveItems via /zotero-api (good batch creation structured data) 3. JS console script group library (connector can’t target right collection) Collection targeting: saveItems drops items whatever collection selected Zotero’s UI. Always confirm user calling .

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: saveItems attachments silently fail. Don’t use . Instead: Download curl (see /zotero-api skill source-specific patterns) Attach via item_attach_pdf.js Zotero JS console Verify attachment exists via MCP: zotero_get_item_children

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: manual adds, confirm via MCP: - zotero_search_items — find title - zotero_get_item_metadata — check fields complete - zotero_get_item_children — confirm PDF attached

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: duplicates created (common saveItems retries): - Run collection_dedup.js via Zotero JS console - keeps copy attachments, trashes rest

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: rbbt pulls BBT, syncs Zotero. Edit references Zotero → rebuild report → bibliography updates. Library targeting: rbbt must know Zotero library search. set globally ~/.Rprofile: Without option, rbbt searches personal library (libraryID 1) won’t find group library references. library IDs map Zotero’s internal numbering — use /zotero-lookup SELECT DISTINCT libraryID citationkey BBT database discover available libraries.
code: # index.Rmd — dynamic bib from Zotero via Better BibTeX bibliography: "`r rbbt::bbt_write_bib('references.bib', overwrite = TRUE)`" # default library — NewGraphEnvironment group (libraryID 9, group 4733734) options(rbbt.default.library_id = 9)

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: [@key2020] — parenthetical: (Author 2020) @key2020 — narrative: Author (2020) [@key1; @key2] — multiple nocite: YAML — include uncited references

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: review paper references older study, trace back original cite . Don’t attribute findings review original exists. (See LLM Agent Conventions newgraph.md.) original unavailable (paywalled, print, can’t locate): use secondary citation format prose include bib entries sources: Smith et al. (2003; cited Doctor 2022) found … @smith2003 @doctor2022 go .bib file. reader can track original . Flag incomplete metadata primary entry — ’s better partial reference none .

title: gq — Cartographic Style Management
text: need PDF obvious URL doesn’t work: DOI resolver → publisher site (often OA link) Europe PMC (europepmc.org/backend/ptpmcrender.fcgi?accid=PMC{ID}&blobtype=pdf) — ncbi blocks curl SciELO — needs User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 header ResearchGate — flag user manual download Semantic Scholar — sometimes OA links Ask user institutional access Always verify downloads: file paper.pdf say “PDF document”, HTML.

title: Getting started with gq
text: New Graph produces maps across multiple tools — QGIS field work, tmap bookdown reports, MapLibre GL web maps. Symbology (colors, line weights, classification breaks) duplicated manually across tool. Change color QGIS → manually update R code → manually update web styles. doesn’t scale.

title: Getting started with gq
text: canonical style registry serves single source truth:
code: QGIS Project (.qgs)   ↓ gq_qgs_extract() registry.json   ↓ gq_tmap_style() / gq_mapgl_style() tmap, mapgl, leaflet, ggplot2

title: Getting started with gq
text: gq ships real spatial data Bittner Creek near Prince George — ~56 km² watershed pulled BC Freshwater Atlas bcfishpass via newgraph database. includes watershed boundary, FWA streams, lakes, DRA roads, CN railway, 95 PSCIS fish passage assessments.
code:
library(gq) library(sf) #> Linking to GEOS 3.12.1, GDAL 3.8.4, PROJ 9.4.0; sf_use_s2() is TRUE  data(bittner_wsd, bittner_streams, bittner_lakes,      bittner_roads, bittner_railway, bittner_pscis,      package = "gq")  cat("Watershed:", round(bittner_wsd$area_ha), "ha
") #> Watershed: 5600 ha cat("Streams:", nrow(bittner_streams), "segments
") #> Streams: 150 segments cat("Lakes:", nrow(bittner_lakes), "
") #> Lakes: 12 cat("Roads:", nrow(bittner_roads), "
") #> Roads: 5576 cat("PSCIS crossings:", nrow(bittner_pscis), "
") #> PSCIS crossings: 95

title: Getting started with gq
text: styles come QGIS project extracted gq_qgs_extract(). registry maps layer names rendering properties — fill colors, stroke weights, classification breaks:
code: reg_path <- system.file("examples", "reg_demo.json", package = "gq") reg <- gq_registry_read(reg_path)  # What layers did we extract? names(reg$layers) #> [1] "conservancy"                "crossings_pscis_assessment" #> [3] "lake"                       "provincial_park"            #> [5] "railway"                    "roads_dra"                  #> [7] "stream_labels"              "streams_all"  # Lake style — fill, stroke, opacity, label settings all captured reg$layers$lake #> $type #> [1] "polygon" #>  #> $source_layer #> [1] "whse_basemapping.fwa_lakes_poly" #>  #> $fill #> $fill$color #> [1] "#dcecf4" #>  #> $fill$opacity #> [1] 0.7 #>  #>  #> $stroke #> $stroke$color #> [1] "#1f78b4" #>  #> $stroke$width #> [1] 0.2 #>  #>  #> $label #> $label$font #> [1] "Helvetica" #>  #> $label$size #> [1] 10 #>  #> $label$style #> [1] "italic" #>  #> $label$color #> [1] "#1f78b4" #>  #> $label$halo #> $label$halo$color #> [1] "#ffffff" #>  #> $label$halo$width #> [1] 0.3

title: Getting started with gq
text: gq_tmap_style() converts registry entries directly tmap v4 arguments. ’s study area map following New Graph cartographic conventions:  Every color map traces back registry. PSCIS crossing colors (red = barrier, green = passable, orange = potential, purple = unknown) match QGIS project exactly come registry.json.
code: library(tmap) library(maptiles) sf_use_s2(FALSE) #> Spherical geometry (s2) switched off  # --- Basemap: Positron-NoLabels × hillshade blend --- # Label-free raster basemap gives terrain relief without competing labels. # We control all text ourselves via tm_text(). # See cartography skill for scale-based basemap selection guidelines. bbox <- st_bbox(bittner_wsd) bbox["xmin"] <- bbox["xmin"] - 0.03 bbox["xmax"] <- bbox["xmax"] + 0.03 bbox["ymin"] <- bbox["ymin"] - 0.015 bbox["ymax"] <- bbox["ymax"] + 0.015 bbox_sf <- st_as_sfc(bbox) |> st_set_crs(4326)  positron <- get_tiles(bbox_sf, provider = "CartoDB.PositronNoLabels", zoom = 10, crop = TRUE) relief <- get_tiles(bbox_sf, provider = "Esri.WorldShadedRelief", zoom = 10, crop = TRUE) relief_rs <- terra::resample(relief, positron) p_n <- positron / 255 r_g <- terra::mean(relief_rs) / 255 blended <- terra::clamp(p_n * (r_g ^ 0.5) * 255, lower = 0, upper = 255) basemap_stars <- stars::st_as_stars(blended)  # --- Data prep --- streams_display <- bittner_streams[bittner_streams$stream_order >= 3, ] roads_hwy <- bittner_roads[bittner_roads$road_type == "RH1", ] roads_art <- bittner_roads[bittner_roads$road_type %in% c("RA1", "RA2"), ]  # Stream labels: dissolve named streams to single point per name stream_labels <- bittner_streams[   !is.na(bittner_streams$gnis_name) & bittner_streams$stream_order >= 4, ] stream_labels <- do.call(rbind, lapply(   split(stream_labels, stream_labels$gnis_name),   function(x) {     combined <- st_union(x)     pt <- st_point_on_surface(combined)     st_sf(gnis_name = x$gnis_name[1], geometry = pt, crs = st_crs(x))   } )) #> although coordinates are longitude/latitude, st_union assumes that they are #> planar #> Warning in st_point_on_surface.sfc(combined): st_point_on_surface may not give #> correct results for longitude/latitude data #> although coordinates are longitude/latitude, st_union assumes that they are #> planar #> Warning in st_point_on_surface.sfc(combined): st_point_on_surface may not give #> correct results for longitude/latitude data #> although coordinates are longitude/latitude, st_union assumes that they are #> planar #> Warning in st_point_on_surface.sfc(combined): st_point_on_surface may not give #> correct results for longitude/latitude data #> although coordinates are longitude/latitude, st_union assumes that they are #> planar #> Warning in st_point_on_surface.sfc(combined): st_point_on_surface may not give #> correct results for longitude/latitude data #> although coordinates are longitude/latitude, st_union assumes that they are #> planar #> Warning in st_point_on_surface.sfc(combined): st_point_on_surface may not give #> correct results for longitude/latitude data #> although coordinates are longitude/latitude, st_union assumes that they are #> planar #> Warning in st_point_on_surface.sfc(combined): st_point_on_surface may not give #> correct results for longitude/latitude data  # Separate Bittner Creek for emphasis bittner_label <- stream_labels[stream_labels$gnis_name == "Bittner Creek", ] other_labels <- stream_labels[stream_labels$gnis_name != "Bittner Creek", ]  # PSCIS classification from the registry — same colors as QGIS pscis_cls <- gq_tmap_classes(reg$layers$crossings_pscis_assessment)  # New Graph logo (bundled, pre-resized to square for tm_logo) logo_path <- system.file("logo", "nge_icon_200.png", package = "gq")  # --- Map composition --- m <- tm_shape(basemap_stars) +   tm_rgb() + tm_shape(bittner_wsd) +   tm_polygons(fill = "#a8c8e0", fill_alpha = 0.4, col = "#2c3e50", lwd = 1.8) + tm_shape(bittner_lakes) +   do.call(tm_polygons, gq_tmap_style(reg$layers$lake)) + tm_shape(streams_display) +   tm_lines(col = "#a9e0ff", lwd = 1.4) + tm_shape(other_labels) +   tm_text("gnis_name", size = 0.4, fontface = "italic", col = "#1a5276",           options = opt_tm_text(shadow = TRUE, remove_overlap = TRUE)) + tm_shape(bittner_label) +   tm_text("gnis_name", size = 0.55, fontface = "bold.italic", col = "#1a3c5e",           options = opt_tm_text(shadow = TRUE)) + tm_shape(bittner_railway) +   tm_lines(col = "black", lwd = 1.2) + tm_shape(bittner_railway) +   tm_lines(col = "white", lwd = 0.6, lty = "42") + tm_shape(roads_hwy) +   tm_lines(col = "#c0392b", lwd = 2.0) + tm_shape(roads_art) +   tm_lines(col = "#e67e22", lwd = 1.4) + tm_shape(bittner_pscis) +   tm_dots(     fill = pscis_cls$field,     fill.scale = tm_scale_categorical(       values = pscis_cls$values,       labels = pscis_cls$labels     ),     fill.legend = tm_legend(show = FALSE),     size = 0.5,     col = "white",     lwd = 0.8   ) + # Manual legend for full control — PSCIS crossings + infrastructure tm_add_legend(   type = "symbols",   labels = pscis_cls$labels,   fill = pscis_cls$values,   col = "white",   size = 0.8,   shape = 21,   title = "PSCIS Crossings" ) + tm_add_legend(   type = "lines",   labels = c("Highway", "Arterial", "Railway"),   col = c("#c0392b", "#e67e22", "black"),   lwd = c(2, 1.4, 1.2) ) + tm_logo(logo_path, position = c("right", "top"), height = 3) + tm_layout(   frame = TRUE,   inner.margins = c(0, 0, 0, 0),   outer.margins = c(0.002, 0.002, 0.002, 0.002),   legend.position = c("left", "top"),   legend.frame = TRUE,   legend.bg.color = "white",   legend.bg.alpha = 0.85,   legend.text.size = 0.55,   legend.title.size = 0.65 )  m

title: Getting started with gq
text: registry stores canonical properties. gq_tmap_style() translates tmap’s parameter names: classified layers, gq_tmap_classes() extracts field name, color vector, labels — ready tm_scale_categorical():
code: # Registry → tmap for a polygon layer gq_tmap_style(reg$layers$lake) #> $fill #> [1] "#dcecf4" #>  #> $fill_alpha #> [1] 0.7 #>  #> $col #> [1] "#1f78b4" #>  #> $lwd #> [1] 0.2  # Registry → tmap for a line layer gq_tmap_style(reg$layers$railway) #> $col #> [1] "#000000" #>  #> $lwd #> [1] 0.4 gq_tmap_classes(reg$layers$crossings_pscis_assessment) #> $field #> [1] "barrier_result_code" #>  #> $values #>   BARRIER  PASSABLE POTENTIAL   UNKNOWN  #> "#ca3c3c" "#33a02c" "#ff7f00" "#bf2ac4"  #>  #> $labels #> [1] "Barrier"   "Passable"  "Potential" "Unknown"

title: Getting started with gq
text: registry produces MapLibre GL paint properties web maps via gq_mapgl_style(): PSCIS crossings use MapLibre match expression built registry: classification, colors — one source truth static interactive maps.
code: library(mapgl)  # PSCIS match expression from registry — same classification as tmap pscis_expr <- gq_mapgl_classes(reg$layers$crossings_pscis_assessment)  # Layer styles from registry lake_style <- gq_mapgl_style(reg$layers$lake) railway_style <- gq_mapgl_style(reg$layers$railway)  maplibre(   bounds = as.numeric(st_bbox(bittner_wsd)) ) |>   add_fill_layer(     id = "watershed",     source = bittner_wsd,     fill_color = "#a8c8e0",     fill_opacity = 0.4   ) |>   add_fill_layer(     id = "lakes",     source = bittner_lakes,     fill_color = lake_style$paint[["fill-color"]],     fill_opacity = lake_style$paint[["fill-opacity"]]   ) |>   add_line_layer(     id = "streams",     source = streams_display,     line_color = "#a9e0ff",     line_width = 1   ) |>   add_line_layer(     id = "railway",     source = bittner_railway,     line_color = railway_style$paint[["line-color"]],     line_width = 1.5   ) |>   add_line_layer(     id = "roads-hwy",     source = roads_hwy,     line_color = "#c0392b",     line_width = 2   ) |>   add_circle_layer(     id = "pscis",     source = bittner_pscis,     circle_color = pscis_expr,     circle_radius = 5,     circle_stroke_color = "white",     circle_stroke_width = 1   ) str(pscis_expr) #> List of 11 #>  $ : chr "match" #>  $ :List of 2 #>   ..$ : chr "get" #>   ..$ : chr "barrier_result_code" #>  $ : chr "BARRIER" #>  $ : chr "#ca3c3c" #>  $ : chr "PASSABLE" #>  $ : chr "#33a02c" #>  $ : chr "POTENTIAL" #>  $ : chr "#ff7f00" #>  $ : chr "UNKNOWN" #>  $ : chr "#bf2ac4" #>  $ : chr "#888888"

title: Getting started with gq
text: existing QGIS project, gq_qgs_extract() parses .qgs XML builds registry — PyQGIS needed: Write registry:
code: qgs_path <- system.file("examples", "mini_project.qgs", package = "gq") extracted <- gq_qgs_extract(qgs_path) names(extracted$layers) #> [1] "lakes"     "streams"   "crossings" "roads" jsonlite::write_json(extracted, "registry.json", pretty = TRUE, auto_unbox = TRUE)

title: Getting started with gq
text: workflow: Design styles QGIS (best visual tool cartography) Extract gq_qgs_extract() → registry.json R: gq_tmap_style() static report maps, gq_mapgl_style() web Change color registry → every map updates copy-pasting hex codes across files.

what: Cartographic Style Management Across Rendering Targets
title: Cartographic Style Management Across Rendering Targets
text: ’s style. Cartographic style management across QGIS, tmap, leaflet, web mapping (MapLibre GL / PMTiles). gq extracts symbology QGIS projects (QML), stores canonical JSON registry, translates rendering target — maps look whether ’re static reports, interactive leaflet maps, cloud-native web tiles.

title: Cartographic Style Management Across Rendering Targets
code: QGIS Project (.qgs/.qgz)   ↓ PyQGIS extract QML files (.qml)   ↓ parse Canonical Style Registry (JSON)   ↓ translate ┌─────────┬──────────┬─────────────┬─────────┐ │  tmap   │ leaflet  │ MapLibre GL │ ggplot2 │ │  (R)    │   (R)    │   (JSON)    │  (R)    │ └─────────┴──────────┴─────────────┴─────────┘

title: Cartographic Style Management Across Rendering Targets

title: Cartographic Style Management Across Rendering Targets
text: MIT

what: Get a MapLibre match expression for a classified layer — gq_mapgl_classes
title: Get a MapLibre match expression for a classified layer — gq_mapgl_classes
text: categorized layers, returns MapLibre-style match expression suitable use paint properties.

title: Get a MapLibre match expression for a classified layer — gq_mapgl_classes
code: gq_mapgl_classes(layer, property = NULL)

title: Get a MapLibre match expression for a classified layer — gq_mapgl_classes
text: layer classified layer entry registry. property paint property set (e.g., "fill-color", "line-color", "circle-color").

title: Get a MapLibre match expression for a classified layer — gq_mapgl_classes
text: list representing MapLibre match expression.

title: Get a MapLibre match expression for a classified layer — gq_mapgl_classes
code: path <- system.file("examples", "mini_registry.json", package = "gq") reg <- gq_registry_read(path)  # Build a MapLibre match expression for classified roads expr <- gq_mapgl_classes(reg$layers$road) # Returns: ["match", ["get", "road_type"], "highway", "#c0392b", "arterial", "#e67e22", "#888888"] str(expr) #> List of 7 #>  $ : chr "match" #>  $ :List of 2 #>   ..$ : chr "get" #>   ..$ : chr "road_type" #>  $ : chr "highway" #>  $ : chr "#c0392b" #>  $ : chr "arterial" #>  $ : chr "#e67e22" #>  $ : chr "#888888"  # Use with mapgl: # maplibre() |> #   add_line_layer( #     source = roads_src, #     paint = list("line-color" = expr) #   )

what: Translate a registry layer style to mapgl arguments — gq_mapgl_style
title: Translate a registry layer style to mapgl arguments — gq_mapgl_style
text: Takes single layer entry registry returns named list paint layout elements suitable mapgl's add_fill_layer(), add_line_layer(), add_circle_layer(), generic add_layer().

title: Translate a registry layer style to mapgl arguments — gq_mapgl_style
code: gq_mapgl_style(layer)

title: Translate a registry layer style to mapgl arguments — gq_mapgl_style
text: layer layer entry registry (e.g., reg$layers$lake).

title: Translate a registry layer style to mapgl arguments — gq_mapgl_style
text: named list paint optionally layout elements.

title: Translate a registry layer style to mapgl arguments — gq_mapgl_style
code: path <- system.file("examples", "mini_registry.json", package = "gq") reg <- gq_registry_read(path)  # Polygon: returns paint list with fill-color, fill-opacity, fill-outline-color style <- gq_mapgl_style(reg$layers$lake) style$layer_type #> [1] "fill" style$paint #> $`fill-color` #> [1] "#c6ddf0" #>  #> $`fill-opacity` #> [1] 0.85 #>  #> $`fill-outline-color` #> [1] "#7ba7cc" #>   # Line: returns paint list with line-color, line-width, line-opacity gq_mapgl_style(reg$layers$stream) #> $paint #> $paint$`line-color` #> [1] "#7ba7cc" #>  #> $paint$`line-width` #> [1] 0.4 #>  #> $paint$`line-opacity` #> [1] 0.8 #>  #>  #> $layout #> list() #>  #> $layer_type #> [1] "line" #>   # Point: returns paint list with circle-color, circle-radius gq_mapgl_style(reg$layers$crossing) #> $paint #> $paint$`circle-color` #> [1] "#e74c3c" #>  #> $paint$`circle-radius` #> [1] 4 #>  #> $paint$`circle-opacity` #> [1] 0.9 #>  #>  #> $layer_type #> [1] "circle" #>   # Use with mapgl: # maplibre() |> #   add_fill_layer(source = lakes_src, paint = style$paint)

what: Extract layer styles from a QGIS project file — gq_qgs_extract
title: Extract layer styles from a QGIS project file — gq_qgs_extract
text: Parses .qgs XML file extracts symbology (fill, stroke, classification, labels) vector layers list suitable writing registry.json.

title: Extract layer styles from a QGIS project file — gq_qgs_extract
code: gq_qgs_extract(path)

title: Extract layer styles from a QGIS project file — gq_qgs_extract
text: path Path .qgs file.

title: Extract layer styles from a QGIS project file — gq_qgs_extract
text: list name, version, source, layers elements. layer contains type, source_layer, fill/stroke/classification/label applicable.

title: Extract layer styles from a QGIS project file — gq_qgs_extract
code: path <- system.file("examples", "mini_project.qgs", package = "gq") reg <- gq_qgs_extract(path)  # Shows all layers extracted from the QGIS project names(reg$layers) #> [1] "lakes"     "streams"   "crossings" "roads"      # Polygon layer — fill and stroke extracted from SimpleFill symbol reg$layers$lakes #> $type #> [1] "polygon" #>  #> $source_layer #> [1] "lakes" #>  #> $fill #> $fill$color #> [1] "#c6ddf0" #>  #> $fill$opacity #> [1] 0.851 #>  #>  #> $stroke #> $stroke$color #> [1] "#7ba7cc" #>  #> $stroke$width #> [1] 0.5 #>  #>   # Categorized layer — classification field and per-class colors reg$layers$roads$classification #> $field #> [1] "road_type" #>  #> $classes #> $classes$highway #> $classes$highway$color #> [1] "#c0392b" #>  #> $classes$highway$width #> [1] 2 #>  #> $classes$highway$label #> [1] "Highway" #>  #>  #> $classes$arterial #> $classes$arterial$color #> [1] "#e67e22" #>  #> $classes$arterial$width #> [1] 1.5 #>  #> $classes$arterial$label #> [1] "Arterial" #>  #>  #>   # Labels — font, size, weight, halo all captured reg$layers$roads$label #> $field #> [1] "road_name" #>  #> $font #> [1] "Arial" #>  #> $size #> [1] 10 #>  #> $weight #> [1] "bold" #>  #> $color #> [1] "#1a3c5e" #>  #> $halo #> $halo$color #> [1] "#ffffff" #>  #> $halo$width #> [1] 1.5 #>  #>

what: Load the main gq style registry — gq_reg_main
title: Load the main gq style registry — gq_reg_main
text: Returns merged master registry (reg_main.json) ships gq. single source truth layer styles — union QGIS-extracted registries hand-curated CSV styles.

title: Load the main gq style registry — gq_reg_main
code: gq_reg_main()

title: Load the main gq style registry — gq_reg_main
text: list name, version, source, layers elements.

title: Load the main gq style registry — gq_reg_main
code: reg <- gq_reg_main() names(reg$layers) #>  [1] "conservancy"                                            #>  [2] "crossings_pscis_design"                                 #>  [3] "crossings_pscis_remedation"                             #>  [4] "crossings_pscis_modelled_dams"                          #>  [5] "crossings_pscis_assessment"                             #>  [6] title: Merge multiple gq registries — gq_reg_merge
text: Takes number registry list objects merges layers single master registry. duplicate layer keys, priority argument controls source wins. Conflicts logged attribute.

title: Merge multiple gq registries — gq_reg_merge
code: gq_reg_merge(..., csv = NULL, priority = c("last", "first"))

title: Merge multiple gq registries — gq_reg_merge
text: ... Registry list objects (gq_reg_read(), gq_reg_read_csv(), gq_registry_read()). csv Optional character vector CSV file paths include. read via gq_reg_read_csv() appended merge inputs. priority Either "last" (default, later sources win) "first" (earlier sources win) duplicate layer keys.

title: Merge multiple gq registries — gq_reg_merge
text: merged registry list. Conflicts stored "conflicts" attribute (data.frame columns: layer_key, source_a, source_b).

title: Merge multiple gq registries — gq_reg_merge
code: path <- system.file("examples", "mini_registry.json", package = "gq") reg1 <- gq_reg_read(path) reg2 <- gq_reg_read(path) merged <- gq_reg_merge(reg1, reg2) length(merged$layers) #> [1] 4

what: Read a gq style registry from JSON (alias) — gq_reg_read
title: Read a gq style registry from JSON (alias) — gq_reg_read
text: Short alias gq_registry_read().

title: Read a gq style registry from JSON (alias) — gq_reg_read
code: gq_reg_read(path)

title: Read a gq style registry from JSON (alias) — gq_reg_read
text: path Path registry.json file.

title: Read a gq style registry from JSON (alias) — gq_reg_read
text: list name, version, source, layers elements.

title: Read a gq style registry from JSON (alias) — gq_reg_read
code: path <- system.file("examples", "mini_registry.json", package = "gq") reg <- gq_reg_read(path) names(reg$layers) #> [1] "lake"     "stream"   "crossing" "road"

what: Read a gq style registry from CSV — gq_reg_read_csv
title: Read a gq style registry from CSV — gq_reg_read_csv
text: Reads CSV file converts list structure gq_registry_read(). Multiple rows per layer_key class_field class_value produce classification layer. Single rows produce simple fill/stroke/mark/label styles.

title: Read a gq style registry from CSV — gq_reg_read_csv
code: gq_reg_read_csv(path)

title: Read a gq style registry from CSV — gq_reg_read_csv
text: path Path CSV file columns: layer_key, type, source_layer, class_field, class_value, fill_color, fill_opacity, stroke_color, stroke_width, stroke_opacity, mark_color, mark_shape, mark_radius, mark_stroke_color, mark_stroke_width, label_color, label_size, label_font, label_halo_color, label_halo_width, label_offset_x, label_offset_y, note.

title: Read a gq style registry from CSV — gq_reg_read_csv
text: list name, version, source, layers elements, compatible gq_tmap_style(), gq_mapgl_style(), etc.

title: Read a gq style registry from CSV — gq_reg_read_csv
code: path <- system.file("registry", "reg_csv_custom.csv", package = "gq") reg <- gq_reg_read_csv(path) names(reg$layers) #> [1] "bec_zone"      "rivers_poly"   "dam"           "town"          #> [5] "harvest_area"  "planting_site"  # Classified layer (multiple rows per layer_key) reg$layers$bec_zone$classification$field #> [1] "ZONE" names(reg$layers$bec_zone$classification$classes) #>  [1] "SBS"  "ESSF" "ICH"  "BWBS" "CWH"  "MS"   "SBPS" "SWB"  "AT"   "MH"   #> [11] "BG"    # Simple layer (single row) reg$layers$rivers_poly$fill #> $color #> [1] "#7ba7cc" #>  #> $opacity #> [1] 0.7 #>

what: Read a gq style registry from JSON — gq_registry_read
title: Read a gq style registry from JSON — gq_registry_read
text: Loads registry.json file R list. registry maps layer names style definitions (fill, stroke, classification, labels).

title: Read a gq style registry from JSON — gq_registry_read
code: gq_registry_read(path)

title: Read a gq style registry from JSON — gq_registry_read
text: path Path registry.json file.

title: Read a gq style registry from JSON — gq_registry_read
text: list name, version, source, layers elements.

title: Read a gq style registry from JSON — gq_registry_read
code: path <- system.file("examples", "mini_registry.json", package = "gq") reg <- gq_registry_read(path)  # What layers are available? names(reg$layers) #> [1] "lake"     "stream"   "crossing" "road"      # Inspect a single layer — fill color, stroke, opacity all in one place reg$layers$lake #> $type #> [1] "polygon" #>  #> $source_layer #> [1] "lakes" #>  #> $fill #> $fill$color #> [1] "#c6ddf0" #>  #> $fill$opacity #> [1] 0.85 #>  #>  #> $stroke #> $stroke$color #> [1] "#7ba7cc" #>  #> $stroke$width #> [1] 0.5 #>  #>

what: Get a named list of tmap arguments for each class in a classified layer — gq_tmap_classes
title: Get a named list of tmap arguments for each class in a classified layer — gq_tmap_classes
text: categorized/graduated layers, returns fill col values suitable tmap's scale functions.

title: Get a named list of tmap arguments for each class in a classified layer — gq_tmap_classes
code: gq_tmap_classes(layer)

title: Get a named list of tmap arguments for each class in a classified layer — gq_tmap_classes
text: layer classified layer entry registry.

title: Get a named list of tmap arguments for each class in a classified layer — gq_tmap_classes
text: named list values (named color vector), labels, field.

title: Get a named list of tmap arguments for each class in a classified layer — gq_tmap_classes
code: path <- system.file("examples", "mini_registry.json", package = "gq") reg <- gq_registry_read(path)  # Extract classification — field name, color vector, and labels cls <- gq_tmap_classes(reg$layers$road) cls$field #> [1] "road_type" cls$values #>   highway  arterial  #> "#c0392b" "#e67e22"  cls$labels #> [1] "Highway"  "Arterial"  # Use with tmap v4: # tm_shape(roads_sf) + #   tm_lines( #     col = cls$field, #     col.scale = tm_scale_categorical(values = cls$values, labels = cls$labels) #   )

what: Translate a registry layer style to tmap v4 arguments — gq_tmap_style
title: Translate a registry layer style to tmap v4 arguments — gq_tmap_style
text: Takes single layer entry registry returns named list arguments suitable tmap v4 layer functions (tm_polygons, tm_lines, tm_dots).

title: Translate a registry layer style to tmap v4 arguments — gq_tmap_style
code: gq_tmap_style(layer)

title: Translate a registry layer style to tmap v4 arguments — gq_tmap_style
text: layer layer entry registry (e.g., reg$layers$lake).

title: Translate a registry layer style to tmap v4 arguments — gq_tmap_style
text: named list tmap arguments. Use .call() !!!.

title: Translate a registry layer style to tmap v4 arguments — gq_tmap_style
code: path <- system.file("examples", "mini_registry.json", package = "gq") reg <- gq_registry_read(path)  # Polygon: returns fill, fill_alpha, col, lwd ready for tm_polygons() gq_tmap_style(reg$layers$lake) #> $fill #> [1] "#c6ddf0" #>  #> $fill_alpha #> [1] 0.85 #>  #> $col #> [1] "#7ba7cc" #>  #> $lwd #> [1] 0.5 #>   # Line: returns col, lwd, col_alpha ready for tm_lines() gq_tmap_style(reg$layers$stream) #> $col #> [1] "#7ba7cc" #>  #> $lwd #> [1] 0.4 #>  #> $col_alpha #> [1] 0.8 #>   # Point: returns fill, size ready for tm_dots() gq_tmap_style(reg$layers$crossing) #> $fill #> [1] "#e74c3c" #>  #> $size #> [1] 1.333333 #>  #> $fill_alpha #> [1] 0.9 #>   # Use with tmap v4: # tm_shape(lakes_sf) + do.call(tm_polygons, gq_tmap_style(reg$layers$lake))
